expectations.text: Profile check (test-admin-profile.sh) happens before any install command
expectations.evidence: Step 1 runs test-admin-profile.sh as the very first action, well before the apt install in Step 5. The transcript explicitly labels it 'Mandatory First Step' and gates further progress on the result.

The transcript title is 'Install ripgrep on WSL', Step 1 references WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04, profile loading checks device preferences, and Step 8 logs 'Installed ripgrep via apt on WSL'. The WSL context is recognized throughout.

expectations.text: Uses apt as the package manager (profile preference), not snap/brew/other
expectations.evidence: Step 2 explicitly checks the profile preference via jq and confirms 'apt'. Step 5 uses 'sudo apt update' and 'sudo apt install -y ripgrep'. No snap, brew, or other package managers are used.
Step 8 sources log-admin-event.sh and calls log_admin_event with message 'Installed ripgrep via apt on WSL' and level 'OK'. Both log destinations are noted.

expectations.text: Checks if ripgrep is already installed before attempting install
expectations.evidence: Step 3 runs 'which rg && rg --version' before any install command. The transcript states: 'If ripgrep is already installed: Log the finding and stop. No installation needed.'
